title: Date-range filtering is broken for the DD/MM/YYYY historical dataset
evidence: src/tools.rs:77-90 compares datetime strings lexically (dt.as_str() < from); novo_campeonato_brasileiro.csv stores dates as DD/MM/YYYY (data.rs:268) while other files use ISO, so date_from/date_to silently mis-filter historical matches
suggestion: Parse dates into a common type before comparison, or normalize DD/MM/YYYY to ISO at load time

title: team_stats/head_to_head double-count overlapping 2012-2019 Brasileirao data
evidence: src/tools.rs:137-175 (team_stats) and 243-289 (head_to_head) scan all matches with no default competition filter; the 2012-2019 Brasileirao appears in both 'Brasileirão Serie A' and 'Brasileirão (Historical)' datasets, inflating W/L/D and goals unless the caller passes a competition filter
suggestion: Deduplicate overlapping competitions (as season_standings already does via prefer_series_a at tools.rs:421), or document that aggregations require a competition filter

title: Tests assert on output substrings rather than computed values
evidence: src/tools.rs:732-824 — e.g. test_team_stats only checks result.contains("Win rate"); no test pins an expected count/score, so logic regressions in aggregation would not be caught
suggestion: Add assertions on known head-to-head or standings values from the fixture data

title: MCP layer is hand-rolled rather than using an MCP SDK crate
evidence: src/mcp.rs defines JsonRpcRequest/Response and tool schemas manually; main.rs:171-192 implements the stdio loop directly
suggestion: Acceptable given immature Rust MCP tooling; noted for cross-run comparison since other runs may use an SDK
